title: Protein and recovery
source: Protein Nutrition for Endurance Athletes, Sports Medicine
text: Protein supports recovery after exercise by driving muscle repair and replacing the amino acids used during training. Endurance athletes generally need more protein than sedentary people, with evidence pointing to around 1.8 grams per kilogram of body weight a day, compared with the general guideline of about 0.8 grams per kilogram. Spreading intake fairly evenly across meals, at roughly 0.3 to 0.4 grams per kilogram each, helps support this repair.
